text:
Once upon a time in a bright little village nestled among whispering forests, there lived a curious girl named Olive. She loved to explore, with wide eyes that could see the most magical things. One evening, after a day of picking raspberries, she noticed something peculiar fluttering among the branches.

It was a gentle creature unlike anything she'd ever seen—a tiny owl with feathers like midnight. Olive knew she had to keep it secret. So every night, before calling bed herself, she winged softly to the owl's cosy nest.

They became friends. The owl would glide close enough for Olive to touch its beak. Olive taught it owl words like pinecone and nightfern. And every moonlit night, they would chirp together songs from different worlds.

One day, an angry farmer discovered their secret. He wanted the owl for himself. But Olive didn't want anything bad to happen to her buddy. So she flew boldly into battle. With swift wings, she scared away the farmer
